title: CT12.N09 completed discharge record — Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?
description: Concrete completed-discharge schema for CT12.N09, the decision node “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?” of tactic CT12 (Peeling loop). It refines the common node-proof record with this node's exact identity, completion state, assigned contract evidence, and any required payload or terminal certificate.

tacticId.description: Framework tactic discriminator for CT12.N09. It must be exactly `CT12`, selecting Peeling loop (loop) as the tactic that owns the decision step “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?” in the exhaustive proof flow.

nodeId.description: Stable graph-node identifier for the obligation “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”. It must be exactly `CT12.N09` so the evidence, dependencies, outgoing branch, and any terminal result are attributed to this step rather than another node.

nodeNumber.description: One-based ordinal of CT12.N09 within tactic CT12 (Peeling loop). It must be exactly 9, matching the N09 suffix and the node's position in that tactic graph.

status.description: Required completion state for CT12.N09. It must be exactly `proved`, meaning that the node's mathematical assertion or construction has been established for the decision obligation “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”; another lifecycle value does not discharge this concrete node.

dischargedContracts.description: Contract identifiers whose proof obligations are discharged at CT12.N09. The array must contain `S-Dich`, `S-Rest`, `S-Pers`; these are precisely the contracts assigned to “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”. Listing an identifier records discharge status but does not replace the matching typed evidence record required in `contractInstances`.

contains.description: Requires `S-Dich` to occur in CT12.N09's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that gives exhaustive, state-measurable alternatives for the residual class for the obligation “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”.

Requires `S-Rest` to occur in CT12.N09's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that restores the declared signature and invariants after a state-changing step for the obligation “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”.

Requires `S-Pers` to occur in CT12.N09's discharged-contract list, because this node must use the contract that proves that the named assertion persists across the recorded state transition for the obligation “Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?”.

contractInstances.description: Typed contract-evidence records that justify CT12.N09 from the recorded branch state. The array must contain at least one instance for every assigned schema: S-Dich (residualClass, alternatives, evidence); S-Rest (fromStateRef, toStateRef, restoredSignature, restoredItems, evidence); S-Pers (fromStateRef, toStateRef, preserved, evidence). Every instance must include its schema discriminator and nonempty evidence as required by the referenced contract definition; together the records must establish the displayed node statement.

schema.description: Contract discriminator for a `S-Dich` evidence instance at CT12.N09. It must be exactly `S-Dich`, identifying the contract that gives exhaustive, state-measurable alternatives for the residual class; the same instance must supply residualClass, alternatives, evidence.

Contract discriminator for a `S-Rest` evidence instance at CT12.N09. It must be exactly `S-Rest`, identifying the contract that restores the declared signature and invariants after a state-changing step; the same instance must supply fromStateRef, toStateRef, restoredSignature, restoredItems, evidence.

Contract discriminator for a `S-Pers` evidence instance at CT12.N09. It must be exactly `S-Pers`, identifying the contract that proves that the named assertion persists across the recorded state transition; the same instance must supply fromStateRef, toStateRef, preserved, evidence.

statement.informal: Prove or verify: Routing theorem — why did restoration fail?

x-obligation.dischargeCriteria: Provide a proof/definition/computation artifact that establishes the displayed statement from the recorded branch state and satisfies every listed contract schema.
